---
title: "{{ TITLE }}"
subtitle: "{{ SUBTITLE }}"
description: "{{ DESCRIPTION }}"  # < 160 chars for SEO/meta
author: "Dr. Tony Dunsworth"
date: "{{ PUBLISH_DATE }}"
date-modified: "{{ MODIFIED_DATE }}"
categories: [911-dispatch, forecasting, staffing, python, r, sql]
tags: [nena, psap, time-series, staffing-model, duckdb, dbt, {{ TAG1 }}, {{ TAG2 }}]
image: "images/posts/{{ SLUG }}-hero.png"
image-alt: "{{ IMAGE_ALT }}"
draft: true
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
number-sections: false
code-fold: show
code-tools: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  freeze: auto  # re-run only when source changes
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    highlight-style: github
    code-links: ["github", "binder"]
    repo-url: https://github.com/tonydunsworth/drddatascience-blog
    repo-actions: [edit, issue]
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.8/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
          <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.8/dist/katex.min.css">
    html-math-method: katex
---

```{=html}
<!-- JSON-LD Schema.org BlogPosting for SEO -->
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "BlogPosting",
  "headline": "{{ TITLE }}",
  "description": "{{ DESCRIPTION }}",
  "author": {"@type": "Person", "name": "Dr. Tony Dunsworth"},
  "datePublished": "{{ PUBLISH_DATE }}",
  "dateModified": "{{ MODIFIED_DATE }}",
  "image": "https://drddatascience.com/images/posts/{{ SLUG }}-hero.png",
  "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Dr. D's Data Science"},
  "mainEntityOfPage": "https://drddatascience.com/blog/{{ SLUG }}.html"
}
</script>
```

## Abstract / TL;DR

> **Problem**: {{ PROBLEM_ONE_LINER }}
>
> **Method**: {{ METHOD_ONE_LINER }}
>
> **Result**: {{ RESULT_ONE_LINER }}
>
> **Artifact**: [GitHub Repo](https://github.com/tonydunsworth/{{ SLUG }}) | [Synthetic Data](https://github.com/tonydunsworth/{{ SLUG }}/data) | [Launch Binder](https://mybinder.org/v2/gh/tonydunsworth/{{ SLUG }}/HEAD)

---

## 1. Introduction {#sec-intro}

*Context for 911 directors, analysts, and data scientists.*

### 1.1 The Pain Point

{{ PAIN_POINT }}

### 1.2 Why Existing Approaches Fall Short

| Approach | Limitation in 911 Context |
|---|---|
| Erlang-C | Stationary arrivals; no covariates; ignores priority mix |
| Simple Moving Average | Lag; no seasonality; no exogenous drivers |
| Prophet (default) | Black box; no priority/zone hierarchy; holiday calendar incomplete |
| Commercial WFM | Expensive; opaque; vendor lock-in; not PSAP-specific |

### 1.3 What You'll Get From This Post

- [ ] **Mental Model**: {{ MENTAL_MODEL }}
- [ ] **Reproducible Code**: `src/{{ SLUG }}/` (Python/R/SQL/Julia/Rust/Go)
- [ ] **Synthetic Data**: `data/synthetic/` — runs immediately
- [ ] **Quarto Chunks**: Drop into your own reports
- [ ] **Decision Framework**: When to use X vs Y

---

## 2. Background & Prior Art {#sec-background}

*2–3 paragraphs + table. Cite NENA standards, key papers, your prior posts.*

### 2.1 Key References

- **NENA Standards**: [NENA-STA-020.1-2023](https://www.nena.org/page/Standards) — Staffing & Workload
- **Academic**: {{ ACADEMIC_REFS }}
- **Industry**: {{ INDUSTRY_REFS }}
- **Prior Posts**: `[[{{ PRIOR_POST_1 }}]]`, `[[{{ PRIOR_POST_2 }}]]`

### 2.2 The Gap This Fills

{{ GAP_FILLED }}

---

## 3. Data & Methods {#sec-methods}

*Code-first. Each subsection has runnable Quarto chunks.*

### 3.1 Data Sources {#sec-data}

```{python}
#| label: load-data
#| echo: true
#| output: false
import duckdb
import pandas as pd

con = duckdb.connect()
# Synthetic data for reproducibility
calls = con.sql("""
  SELECT * FROM read_parquet('data/synthetic/calls_*.parquet')
""").df()

print(f"Shape: {calls.shape}")
print(f"Date range: {calls['call_time'].min()} to {calls['call_time'].max()}")
print(f"Zones: {calls['zone'].nunique()}")
print(f"Priorities: {calls['priority'].unique()}")
```

```{r}
#| label: load-data-r
#| echo: true
library(tidyverse)
library(tsibble)
library(duckdb)

con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb())
calls <- dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT * FROM read_parquet('data/synthetic/calls_*.parquet')")
calls_ts <- calls |>
  mutate(datetime = as.POSIXct(call_time)) |>
  as_tsibble(index = datetime, key = c(zone, priority))
```

```{sql}
--| label: feature-eng-sql
--| echo: true
--| output: false
-- DuckDB feature engineering (also in dbt: models/features/call_features.sql)
SELECT
  date_trunc('hour', call_time) AS hour,
  zone,
  priority,
  COUNT(*) AS call_volume,
  AVG(CASE WHEN priority IN ('E','D') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_acuity_pct,
  -- Exogenous: weather, holidays, events
  CASE WHEN EXTRACT(dow FROM call_time) IN (0,6) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_weekend
FROM raw_calls
GROUP BY 1,2,3
```

### 3.2 Exploratory Analysis {#sec-eda}

```{python}
#| label: eda-volume
#| echo: true
#| fig-cap: "Hourly call volume by zone (synthetic)"
#| fig-alt: "Heatmap showing hourly call volume patterns across 5 PSAP zones"
import plotly.express as px

hourly = calls.groupby([calls['call_time'].dt.hour, 'zone']).size().reset_index(name='volume')
fig = px.density_heatmap(hourly, x='call_time', y='zone', z='volume',
                         color_continuous_scale='Viridis')
fig.show()
```

```{r}
#| label: eda-seasonality
#| echo: true
library(feasts)
library(ggplot2)

calls_ts |>
  filter(zone == "ZONE_1") |>
  gg_season(call_volume, period = "day") +
  labs(title = "Daily Seasonality: Zone 1", y = "Call Volume")
```

**Key Patterns Observed**:
- {{ PATTERN_1 }}
- {{ PATTERN_2 }}
- {{ PATTERN_3 }}

### 3.3 Modeling Approach {#sec-modeling}

> **Problem Type**: {{ PROBLEM_TYPE }} | **Target**: {{ TARGET }} | **Horizon**: {{ HORIZON }}

| Component | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Algorithm** | {{ ALGORITHM }} | {{ ALGO_RATIONALE }} |
| **Features** | Temporal + Spatial + Exogenous | {{ FEATURE_RATIONALE }} |
| **Validation** | {{ VALIDATION }} | {{ VALID_RATIONALE }} |
| **Uncertainty** | {{ UNCERTAINTY_METHOD }} | {{ UNCERT_RATIONALE }} |
| **Baseline** | {{ BASELINE }} | {{ BASELINE_RATIONALE }} |

#### 3.3.1 Python: LightGBM + SHAP

```{python}
#| label: model-lgbm
#| echo: true
#| output: true
import lightgbm as lgb
from sklearn.model_selection import TimeSeriesSplit
import shap

# Feature matrix
X = features.drop('call_volume', axis=1)
y = features['call_volume']

# Walk-forward CV
tscv = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=5, test_size=168)  # 1 week ahead
scores = []
for train_idx, test_idx in tscv.split(X):
    X_train, X_test = X.iloc[train_idx], X.iloc[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y.iloc[train_idx], y.iloc[test_idx]
    
    model = lgb.LGBMRegressor(
        objective='poisson',
        n_estimators=500,
        learning_rate=0.05,
        num_leaves=64,
        random_state=42
    )
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, eval_set=[(X_test, y_test)], callbacks=[lgb.early_stopping(50)])
    pred = model.predict(X_test)
    scores.append(mean_absolute_error(y_test, pred))

print(f"Walk-forward MAE: {np.mean(scores):.2f} ± {np.std(scores):.2f}")

# SHAP explainability
explainer = shap.TreeExplainer(model)
shap_values = explainer.shap_values(X_test)
shap.summary_plot(shap_values, X_test, plot_type="bar")
```

#### 3.3.2 R: fable + NeuralProphet

```{r}
#| label: model-fable
#| echo: true
library(fable)
library(fable.prophet)

fit <- calls_ts |>
  model(
    prophet = prophet(call_volume ~ season(period=168, order=3) + 
                      season(period=24, order=6) + 
                      holiday(us_holidays) + 
                      weather_temp + is_event),
    lgbm = LGBM(call_volume ~ .)
  )

fc <- fit |> forecast(h = "1 week")
accuracy(fc) |>
  filter(.model == "prophet") |>
  select(RMSE, MAE, MAPE)
```

#### 3.3.3 Julia: JuMP + HiGHS (Staffing Optimization)

```{julia}
#| label: model-julia
#| echo: true
using JuMP, HiGHS, DataFrames, CSV

# Data: forecasted volume per zone per hour
df = CSV.read("outputs/forecast_zone_hour.csv", DataFrame)

model = Model(HiGHS.Optimizer)
@variable(model, x[z in zones, h in hours] >= 0, Int)  # staff per zone per hour

# Coverage constraint: staff >= required (from Erlang-C or ML)
@constraint(model, [z=zones, h=hours], x[z,h] >= required[z,h])

# Shift constraints (8-hr shifts, min 2 consecutive days off)
# ... shift generation logic ...

# Objective: minimize total staff (or cost with OT premium)
@objective(model, Min, sum(cost[z] * x[z,h] for z in zones, h in hours))

optimize!(model)
println("Optimal staff: $(objective_value(model))")
```

#### 3.3.4 Rust: High-Perf Feature Pipeline (polars)

```{rust}
//| label: model-rust
//| echo: true
use polars::prelude::*;
use polars::lazy::dsl::*;

let lf = LazyCsvReader::new("data/raw/calls.csv")
    .with_has_header(true)
    .finish()?;

// Temporal features
let lf = lf
    .with_columns([
        col("call_time").dt().hour().alias("hour"),
        col("call_time").dt().weekday().alias("dow"),
        col("call_time").dt().month().alias("month"),
        when(col("priority").is_in(["E","D"])).then(lit(1)).otherwise(lit(0)).alias("high_acuity"),
    ])
    .group_by([col("zone"), col("hour"), col("dow")])
    .agg([
        col("call_time").count().alias("volume"),
        col("high_acuity").mean().alias("acuity_rate"),
    ]);

// Write parquet for Python/R/Julia consumption
lf.collect()?.write_parquet("data/features/zone_hour_dow.parquet")?;
```

#### 3.3.5 Go: Production Serving (gonum + chi)

```{go}
//| label: model-go
//| echo: true
package main

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "net/http"
    "github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
    "gonum.org/v1/gonum/stat"
)

type ForecastRequest struct {
    Zone string `json:"zone"`
    Hours int `json:"hours"`
}

type ForecastResponse struct {
    Predictions []float64 `json:"predictions"`
    Lower []float64 `json:"lower"`
    Upper []float64 `json:"upper"`
}

func forecastHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    var req ForecastRequest
    json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req)
    
    // Load LightGBM model (via CGO or ONNX)
    preds, lower, upper := serveModel(req.Zone, req.Hours)
    
    json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(ForecastResponse{
        Predictions: preds, Lower: lower, Upper: upper,
    })
}

func main() {
    r := chi.NewRouter()
    r.Post("/api/v1/forecast", forecastHandler)
    http.ListenAndServe(":8080", r)
}
```

---

## 4. Results {#sec-results}

*Figures, tables, and practical interpretation.*

### 4.1 Forecast Accuracy

```{python}
#| label: results-table
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Walk-forward validation: MAE by horizon and zone"
#| tbl-label: tbl-accuracy
import pandas as pd
results = pd.read_csv("outputs/validation_results.csv")
print(results.to_markdown())
```

| Model | Horizon | Zone | MAE | RMSE | CRPS | vs Baseline |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| LightGBM | 1 week | All | {{ MAE_LGBM }} | {{ RMSE_LGBM }} | {{ CRPS_LGBM }} | {{ IMPROVEMENT }}% |
| NeuralProphet | 1 week | All | {{ MAE_NP }} | {{ RMSE_NP }} | {{ CRPS_NP }} | {{ IMPROVEMENT_NP }}% |
| Seasonal Naïve | 1 week | All | {{ MAE_SN }} | {{ RMSE_SN }} | {{ CRPS_SN }} | — |

### 4.2 Staffing Impact Simulation

```{python}
#| label: staffing-sim
#| echo: true
#| fig-cap: "Projected overtime reduction vs. current schedule"
#| fig-alt: "Bar chart comparing current OT hours vs. optimized schedule across 4 weeks"
import plotly.graph_objects as go

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(name='Current Schedule', x=weeks, y=current_ot))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(name='Optimized', x=weeks, y=optimized_ot))
fig.update_layout(barmode='group', yaxis_title='OT Hours/Week')
fig.show()
```

**Key Findings**:
- **{{ FINDING_1 }}** (e.g., 23% OT reduction, 95% coverage maintained)
- **{{ FINDING_2 }}** (e.g., Zone 3 consistently understaffed 22:00–06:00)
- **{{ FINDING_3 }}** (e.g., Weather covariates add 4% MAE improvement)

---

## 5. Practical Takeaways for PSAPs {#sec-takeaways}

| Role | Immediate Action | Tool/Template |
|---|---|---|
| **Director** | {{ DIR_ACTION }} | {{ DIR_TOOL }} |
| **Analyst** | {{ ANALYST_ACTION }} | {{ ANALYST_TOOL }} |
| **IT/Dev** | {{ DEV_ACTION }} | {{ DEV_TOOL }} |
| **Trainer** | {{ TRAIN_ACTION }} | {{ TRAIN_TOOL }} |

### 5.1 Quick-Start Checklist

1. [ ] Clone repo: `git clone https://github.com/tonydunsworth/{{ SLUG }}`
2. [ ] Install env: `uv sync` / `renv::restore()` / `pkg> instantiate`
3. [ ] Run synthetic: `python src/{{ SLUG }}/run_synthetic.py`
4. [ ] Swap data: Replace `data/synthetic/` with your CAD export (schema in `docs/schema.md`)
5. [ ] Retrain: `python src/{{ SLUG }}/train.py --config config/prod.yaml`
6. [ ] Schedule: Add `src/{{ SLUG }}/predict.py` to cron/Airflow/dbt

---

## 6. Limitations & Future Work {#sec-limitations}

| Limitation | Mitigation | Future Work |
|---|---|---|
| {{ LIM_1 }} | {{ MIT_1 }} | {{ FUT_1 }} |
| {{ LIM_2 }} | {{ MIT_2 }} | {{ FUT_2 }} |
| {{ LIM_3 }} | {{ MIT_3 }} | {{ FUT_3 }} |

**Honest Assessment**: {{ HONEST_ASSESSMENT }}

---

## 7. Reproducibility Package {#sec-repro}

| Component | Location | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **Code** | `github.com/tonydunsworth/{{ SLUG }}` | ☐ Public ☐ Private |
| **Data** | `data/synthetic/` + access request | ☐ Synthetic only ☐ Real (anonymized) |
| **Environment** | `requirements.txt` / `renv.lock` / `Project.toml` / `Cargo.lock` / `go.mod` | ☐ Locked |
| **Quarto Render** | `quarto render {{ SLUG }}.qmd` | ☐ Passes |
| **CI/CD** | GitHub Actions → Netlify | ☐ Green |

```bash
# One-command reproduction
git clone https://github.com/tonydunsworth/{{ SLUG }}.git
cd {{ SLUG }}
uv sync && python src/train.py --config config/synthetic.yaml
quarto render {{ SLUG }}.qmd --to html
```

---

## References {#sec-refs}

::: {#refs}
:::

---

## Appendix: Full Code Listings {#sec-appendix}

### A.1 Complete Training Pipeline (`src/{{ SLUG }}/train.py`)

```{python}
#| label: appendix-train
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
# <<< src/{{ SLUG }}/train.py >>>
```

### A.2 dbt Feature Model (`models/features/call_features.sql`)

```{sql}
--| label: appendix-dbt
--| echo: true
--| eval: false
-- <<< models/features/call_features.sql >>>
```

### A.3 Quarto Chunk Library (`chunks/{{ SLUG }}-chunks.qmd`)

```{markdown}
#| label: appendix-chunks
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
# Drop these into any Quarto doc
```

---

*Published: {{ PUBLISH_DATE }} | Updated: {{ MODIFIED_DATE }} | [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/tonydunsworth/drddatascience-blog/edit/main/posts/{{ SLUG }}.qmd) | [Report Issue](https://github.com/tonydunsworth/drddatascience-blog/issues/new?template=post-feedback.yml&title={{ SLUG }})*

---

**License**: Code — MIT | Content — CC-BY-4.0 | Data — Synthetic (CC0) / Request Access
